components.desc: Create src/tests/fib.fs, src/tests/fizzbuzz.fs, src/tests/stackops.fs verbatim from spec, and src/tests/golden/{fib,fizzbuzz,stackops}.out with the exact pinned bytes (number lines end in one trailing space; fizzbuzz word lines do not; goldens written from spec, never regenerated from program output). check.sh: verify all 6 files exist, verify golden byte counts/contents against embedded expected bytes (e.g. via cmp against a here-doc), exit nonzero on any mismatch.

src/stage0/stage0.py: python3-stdlib-only Forth interpreter core. CLI: python3 src/stage0/stage0.py <prog.fs> [input.fs]. Tokenizer (whitespace-split, case-insensitive), int data stack, signed decimal literals, arithmetic (+ - * / mod negate), comparison (= < > with -1/0), stack ops (dup drop swap over rot), output (. with exactly one trailing space no newline, emit, cr, ." text"), colon definitions, nested control flow inside definitions (if/else/then, begin/until, do/loop/i where do takes limit start), variable/@/!, comments (backslash-to-EOL and inline parens). check.sh: run small inline .fs snippets covering each word class and cmp output against expected bytes, exit 0 only if all pass.

Extend src/stage0/stage0.py with compiler-support words backed by an interpreter-side string table with integer handles (handle 0 invalid): token (reads next whitespace token from second-argv input file, pushes handle or 0 at EOF), h. (print handle string no newline), h= (string equality flag), h>n (parse int: n -1 on success else 0 0), s" text" (literal string handle, works inside and outside definitions). check.sh: feed a driver .fs plus an input .fs exercising token/h./h=/h>n/s" including EOF behavior and parse failure, cmp against expected bytes.

Verify stage0 interprets all three pinned tests byte-exact (acceptance criterion 1). check.sh: for each of fib, fizzbuzz, stackops run python3 src/stage0/stage0.py src/tests/<t>.fs and cmp against src/tests/golden/<t>.out; exit nonzero on any diff.

src/stage1/compiler.fs: Forth-only program (zero Python in file) run by stage0 that reads a Forth program via token from argv[2] and writes an equivalent standalone Python program to stdout: first emits a Python runtime preamble (int stack + word implementations for all Stage 0 words except compiler-support words), then translates the input token stream (literals, colon definitions, if/else/then, begin/until, do/loop/i, variables/@/!, ./emit/cr/.", comments, arithmetic/comparison/stack ops) into Python. check.sh: compile a small non-pinned smoke program, run emitted .py, cmp its output against the same program run interpreted under stage0.

src/gate/check.sh: stage-2 equivalence gate. For EACH of fib, fizzbuzz, stackops: (1) run interpreted via stage0 and diff against golden; (2) compile via python3 src/stage0/stage0.py src/stage1/compiler.fs src/tests/<t>.fs > build-out/<t>.py; (3) run python3 build-out/<t>.py and diff against same golden. Byte-exact cmp/diff, any mismatch exits nonzero. Non-interactive, under 60s, no network.

Stretch, must not block build. Attempt self-hosting: extend stage1's emitted runtime to include compiler-support words (string table, token reading argv[1] of the emitted program), then: python3 src/stage0/stage0.py src/stage1/compiler.fs src/stage1/compiler.fs > compiler.py; python3 compiler.py src/tests/fib.fs > fib2.py; cmp fib2.py output against fib golden. src/selfhost/check.sh MUST exit 0 regardless of outcome and write honest result (achieved / failed with WHY) to src/selfhost/RESULT.md. Do not fake success.
